description_long: This is a small, twin-engine propeller airplane with a low-wing configuration and a conventional tail. The fuselage is primarily white with visible weathering and some red and blue markings, including a registration number on the tail. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and features multiple windows, suggesting a multi-seat, enclosed design. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel, all of which appear fixed and exposed. The wings are straight and lack winglets, and the engines are mounted on the wings. The airplane is situated on a concrete surface, possibly an apron or parking area, with visible shadows and some ground debris.
description: A weathered, white twin-engine propeller airplane with a low-wing design, tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail, parked on a concrete surface.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the cockpit windows, nose, and both engines mounted on the wings, with the tail and registration number visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail section, both vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the back of the wings and engines.
description_view_2: Left-rear view focusing on the tail, left wing, and the exposed landing gear, with the fuselage and cockpit partially visible.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the nose, cockpit windows, both propeller engines, and the tricycle landing gear arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no winglets or visible dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle, fixed landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
